items.summary: McConnell, R-Ky., said he would return to the Senate but didn’t give a date.

items.title: Here’s the biggest news you missed this weekend
items.summary: Senator Lindsey Graham died at 71. The United States launched a round of retaliatory strikes against Iran. Wimbledon crowns new champions.

items.title: Jorge Valdano elige a los finalistas del Mundial 2026 en Rumbo a la Gran Final
items.summary: Jorge Valdano analiza las semifinales del Mundial 2026, compara a Francia, España, Argentina e Inglaterra y revela cuáles serán, para él, las dos selecciones que disputarán la gran final.

items.title: Georgia's unique in the American Revolution told through its historical markers
items.summary: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Andrew Iden, Executive Producer of Marked!: The Podcast , which examines Georgia's role in America's revolutionary period through the 2,000 roadside historical marker.

items.title: Philadelphia area digs out from damage left by a severe series of microburst storms
items.summary: Residents of Philadelphia and some surrounding counties are cleaning up and assessing damage after a series of severe storms called microbursts swept through with winds up to 60 or 70 miles per hour

items.title: Fire breaks out at a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people, officials say
items.summary: Footage shared online by first responders shows a huge blaze raging and plumes coming out of the front door of the pub in the northern part of the Thai capital.

items.title: Toronto police looking for suspects after deadly shooting at festival
items.summary: At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.

items.title: What to know about the cyclosporiasis outbreak hitting more than half of U.S. states
items.summary: A surge of cases of the intestinal illness that causes diarrhea and nausea has been detected in 31 states, according to federal health authorities, but the source is still under investigation.

items.title: Graham makes final remarks in Ukraine a day before this death
items.summary: Sen. Lindsey Graham met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and made public remarks during a trip to Kyiv just one day before his death. Graham said he was focused on advancing legislation aimed at imposing sanctions on Russian oil.

items.title: Adam Schiff remembers Lindsey Graham as ‘larger than life’: Full interview
items.summary: Sen. Adam Schiff (D-Calif.) joins Meet the Press to reflect on the passing of his colleague Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) and discuss the U.S. war with Iran as both countries continue to trade strikes.

items.title: Innovation, data fixes fuel Native American graduation gains at federally funded schools
items.summary: The U.S. agency that oversees dozens of schools serving Native Americans is reporting more on-time high school graduations than ever

items.title: Georgia teen charged in Apalachee High School shooting to appear in court for plea and sentencing
items.summary: A judge has set a plea and sentencing hearing for a teenager accused of killing four people in a 2024 shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia

items.title: Tim Scott remembers how Lindsey Graham ‘committed to the people he loved’: Full interview
items.summary: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Sen. Tim Scott (R-S.C.) reacts to the death of his fellow South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham (R).

items.title: Netanyahu says he's lost a 'beloved friend' after the death of Lindsey Graham: Full interview
items.summary: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu remembers the late Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) for his “clarity… courage… and convictions.”

items.title: Trump says ‘I have somebody that I like’ to succeed Lindsey Graham but ‘too soon’ to say who
items.summary: President Donald Trump joins Meet the Press in an exclusive interview and weighs in on the decision Gov. Henry McMaster (R-S.C.) now faces to appoint someone to fill the late Sen. Lindsey Graham’s (R-S.C.) seat. “Ultimately they'll have an election,” Trump said. “Some are good, some are bad. And but I have somebody that I like, but I'm not going to tell you who now because it's too soon.”

items.title: Trump remembers his last call with Lindsey Graham the night before his death: Full interview
items.summary: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, President Donald Trump reacts to the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.).

items.title: A Bible passage is at the center of a debate over how the U.S. should treat immigrants
items.summary: A debate over the Bible verse Matthew 25 is pitting mainline pastors, Black protestants and the pope against evangelical politicians put on the defensive over President Trump's policies.

items.title: Special Report: Looking back on the political legacy of Sen. Lindsey Graham
items.summary: Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., who had held his Senate seat since 2003 and was seen as a war hawk and significant voice on defense and foreign policy, has died at the age of 71 from a “sudden illness.” NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin and Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker report on Graham’s rise in Congress, his relationship with President Donald Trump, and his views on the wars in Iran and between Russia and Ukraine.

items.title: A promising tale from Senegal of fish, rice .... and dangerous worms
items.summary: Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.

items.title: Campaign text messages could soon get more effective — and annoying
items.summary: Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages, making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.

items.title: An artist brought 'I.C.E. pops' to a Texas campus. The show was shut down in days
items.summary: The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.

items.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
items.summary: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.

items.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina, Trump ally and foreign policy hawk, dies at 71
items.summary: His office said Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness." The Republican senator was instrumental in enacting Trump's policy and staffing priorities.

items.title: Argentina elimina a Suiza y así reaccionaron los protagonistas del partido
items.summary: Lionel Scaloni destacó el esfuerzo de Argentina. Julián Álvarez, Lautaro Martínez, José Manuel López y Leandro Paredes compartieron sus sensaciones, mientras Gregor Kobel y Murat Yakin lamentaron la eliminación de Suiza.

items.title: Scaloni tras el pase a Semifinal ante Inglaterra: "Es un partido de fútbol, no hay más que eso"
items.summary: Pese al enorme peso histórico del duelo de Argentina ante los ingleses y las Malvinas de por medio, el DT Campeón del Mundo apuntó solo a lo futbolístico; además, dio mérito a sus jugadores tras el triunfo ante Suiza, explicó el gol de Mac Allister y más.

items.title: Radar Mundial: Inglaterra y Argentina completan unas semifinales de lujo
items.summary: Inglaterra eliminó a Noruega con un doblete de Jude Bellingham y Argentina venció a Suiza con goles de Mac Allister, Julián Álvarez y Lautaro Martínez. Por primera vez, las cuatro mejores selecciones del ranking FIFA jugarán las semifinales.

items.title: Argentina ya está en semifinales y los expertos también analizan el triunfo de Inglaterra
items.summary: Nuestros expertos analizaron la clasificación de Argentina a las semifinales de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026 tras vencer a Suiza. Además, repasaron el triunfo de Inglaterra sobre Noruega y las claves que marcaron el encuentro.

items.title: Lionel Messi vs. Suiza: cámara exclusiva de todas sus acciones
items.summary: El capitán argentino no marcó gol, pero fue el orquestador de la albiceleste que tuvo un sufrido encuentro contra Suiza y aquí te traemos todas sus acciones.

items.title: Los golazos que se registraron en los duelos de cuartos de final es lo mejor del día
items.summary: La jornada sabatina de cuartos de final protagonizó lindos momentos en los duelos entre Inglaterra vs Noruega y Argentina frente a Suiza.

items.title: El show de Scaloni en la banca: conmovido y sin festejar en el triunfo de Argentina ante Suiza
items.summary: Conmovido y contenido, el estratega se guardó los festejos en cada gol de la "Albiceleste" ante Suiza en cuartos de final y al pitazo final se marchó rápido al vestuario con un abrazo a Maxi Rodríguez. Revive cómo vivió cada momento desde la banca.

items.title: Lautaro Martínez: “Vamos con un equipo de mucha jerarquía”
items.summary: El delantero argentino señala que lo importantes es recuperar energías y preparar el próximo partido que será aún más desafiante.

items.title: López tras triunfo de Argentina: "Es un partido especial; estamos preparados para lo que viene"
items.summary: El delantero argentino habló tras el triunfo ante Suiza y de cara a la Semifinal ante Inglaterra, compartió sus sensaciones del duelo en Kansas City y lo que espera del partido que lleva una gran carga histórica.

items.title: Martha Lillard, last US polio patient using iron lung, dies at 78 in Oklahoma
items.summary: Martha Lillard, the last U.S. polio patient using an iron lung, has died at 78 in Oklahoma

items.title: Iran’s supreme leader vows revenge for father’s killing
items.summary: Iranian state media reports that the Revolutionary Guard says the Strait of Hormuz is closed until further notice. It comes as Iran’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is vowing revenge for his father’s killing, saying it is the “demand” of his country. Khamenei has not been seen since he was severely injured when the war began. His father was killed early in the war by U.S. and Israeli air strikes. NBC News’ Julie Tsirkin has the latest.

items.title: Democrat announces whistleblower allegations of construction problems at Kennedy Center
items.summary: A Democratic senator says whistleblowers have alleged numerous problems with rushed reconstruction of the Kennedy Center

items.title: Mexican builder fatally shot by an ICE officer is mourned after making a life in the US
items.summary: The builder got up every morning long before dawn, left home to pick up his construction crew and then headed out to work on yet another house somewhere across the sprawl of Houston

items.title: Judge tosses remnants of Proud Boys seditious conspiracy case after Trump's broad clemency
items.summary: A federal judge has dismissed the remnants of the government’s landmark case against far-right Proud Boys members who were convicted of seditious conspiracy

items.title: New York Times reporters are subpoenaed after Air Force One reporting, newspaper says
items.summary: The New York Times reports the Trump administration has subpoenaed several of its journalists after their report over security concerns with the new Air Force One

items.title: A July 4 boat trip, an 18-year-old's death and a family's search for answers in the Deep South
items.summary: A week ago, 18-year-old Nolan Xavier Wells took a boat trip with friends to celebrate the Fourth of July on an island off Mississippi’s Gulf Coast

items.title: As gas plants rise to power AI, renewable energy allies are fighting for cleaner alternatives
items.summary: Renewable energy allies are trying to ensure that massive data centers will be powered by climate-friendly sources

items.title: U.S. says Strait of Hormuz is open despite Iran declaring it closed as strikes continue
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump threatened Iran after the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei saw open calls for his killing.

items.title: Trump threatens Iran after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral saw open calls for his killing
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened Iran after the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei saw open calls for his killing

items.title: Rapidly growing wildfire in Los Angeles County prompts evacuations in remote high desert
items.summary: A fast-spreading blaze has prompted evacuations in a sparsely populated area of Los Angeles County

items.title: Trump administration rolls back a key protection for imperiled wildlife
items.summary: The Trump administration has finalized a rule that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act

items.title: Emergency crews rescue about 20 after campground building collapses in flooding
items.summary: Emergency crews in Missouri rescued about 20 people on Friday after a building collapsed at a campground

items.title: Both engines flamed out before small jet crashed in June on highway, NTSB report says
items.summary: Both engines flamed out on a small business jet that crashed on a Texas highway in June, killing one person and injuring six, preventing the pilots from being able to reach the Laredo airport

items.title: County seeks help to care for 16 siblings from squalid home, prosecute their family
items.summary: The discovery of 16 siblings who authorities say were held at a home for years in squalid conditions is straining their rural Ohio county’s resources as it works to prosecute their parents and two grandparents and address the needs of so many children ...

items.title: We want to hear about your experience with gambling addiction
items.summary: If you or a loved one has been affected by a gambling problem, NBC News wants to hear from you.

items.title: Wally Funk, aviation pioneer who was the oldest woman to travel into space, dies at 87
items.summary: Wally Funk, an aviation pioneer who was the oldest woman to launch into space, has died

items.title: Mexican man killed in Houston ICE shooting was not the target of operation, lawmaker says
items.summary: A Texas congresswoman says a Mexican man living in the U.S. who was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent was not the person federal authorities had been targeting in a Houston operation

items.title: Lawyers for man charged with killing Charlie Kirk question reliability of evidence
items.summary: Lawyers for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk plan to call a final witness on Friday as they try to raise doubts about the prosecution’s case

items.title: As the country turns 250, retired judges hit the road to defend judicial independence
items.summary: A group of retired federal and state judges has been barnstorming through Ohio and Pennsylvania on the nation's 250th anniversary to defend judicial independence and bolster the rule of law

items.title: Man charged with killing National Guard member hospitalized after refusing food
items.summary: Federal prosecutors say a man accused of shooting two National Guard troops near the White House, killing one of them, has been taken to a hospital for treatment of a potentially life-threatening medical condition after he refused to eat food in jail

items.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit challenging Tuberville's eligibility to run for Alabama governor
items.summary: A judge has dismissed a lawsuit alleging U.S. Sen. Tommy Tuberville does not meet the residency requirement to run for governor of Alabama

items.title: Former Olympian pleads not guilty in Reflecting Pool damage case
items.summary: A former Olympic canoe racer has pleaded not guilty to deliberately damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool

items.title: Death toll from Tropical Storm Maysak in southern China jumps to 39
items.summary: Death toll from Tropical Storm Maysak in southern China jumps to 39

items.title: Trump's plan for a triumphal arch in the nation's capital is getting another review
items.summary: President Donald Trump's plan to build a triumphal arch that would alter the Washington, D.C., skyline is getting another review from the federal agency whose approval he needs to build it

items.title: New Year's Eve ball drops in Times Square for America's 250th
items.summary: The New Year's Eve ball in Times Square has dropped for America's 250th anniversary, making this the first time the ball has dropped outside of a New Year's celebration.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
